hypothesis: IF we run `python -W default` import sweep over helen_os core modules AND pytest over both test trees, THEN we can enumerate every currently-suppressed warning and each collection error, and assign a disposition.

experiment.action_a: python -W default import of 10 helen_os.* modules
experiment.action_c: pytest -W default -rw helen_os_scaffold/tests/ (939 collected, 1 collection error)

helen_os_core_imports.note: Clean. All 10 kernel modules import under -W error::DeprecationWarning|SyntaxWarning|PendingDeprecationWarning without emitting or raising.

helen_os_test_suite.note: 373/374 pass, 1 pre-existing SEAM-001 C12 blocker failure, zero warnings.

warnings.upstream: Pydantic V2 migration — V1 @validator to be removed in V3
Pydantic V2 migration — V1 @validator to be removed in V3

collection_error.origin: helen_os/integrations/airi_bridge.py:28 imports `from helen.router import route_input` which does not resolve in the current tree
collection_error.impact: 1 test file uncollectable; 939 tests still collected around it

dispositions.airi_bridge_websockets_warnings: DEFER — websockets 14+ rename requires upgrading airi_bridge.py to the new async interface. Non-kernel. No current test failure. Separate tranche; schedule for when airi_bridge is exercised in production (currently test_airi_bridge.py does not collect due to helen.router gap).
dispositions.do_next_pydantic_v1_validators: DEFER — migrating @validator → @field_validator is mechanical but touches the /do_next API request model, which is load-bearing for Knowledge Compiler V2 ratification. Bundling this with the V2 ratification work avoids churn. Warnings are noisy but non-breaking until Pydantic V3.
dispositions.test_airi_bridge_collection_error: DEFER — root cause is the unresolved `helen.router` import. Either (a) the router package needs to land on main, or (b) airi_bridge.py's dependency on it should become optional. Either direction is out of scope for a warnings audit. Test file currently does not collect, so it does not count toward the 373/374 pass rate; treated as 'unreachable', not 'failing'.

regression_check.rationale: Zero mutations. Pure audit. Scaffold suite state is recorded for completeness but is a separate test corpus.

close_conditions: every warning has a documented disposition
reopen_conditions: websockets or Pydantic pushes V3 that removes compatibility shim
helen.router module appears on main — then test_airi_bridge.py should be re-evaluated

failure_model.false_positive: Upstream package re-emits warnings from its own code even when our usage is current. Mitigated by listing origin (`our code` vs `transitive`).
failure_model.false_negative: Warnings suppressed by `warnings.filterwarnings` or `-W ignore` in conftest. Not observed here; -W default overrides project-level filters.
failure_model.gameable: An author could add `warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')` to silence a warning without fixing the root cause. Not observed.
failure_model.breaks_if: Pydantic V3 drops V1 @validator (removes compatibility shim) — do_next.py:48/55 become hard errors.

note: E18 finding: helen_os/ kernel is warning-clean. Non-kernel scaffold has 4 DeprecationWarnings (2 websockets, 2 Pydantic V1-style) and 1 collection error (missing helen.router module). All three findings are deferrable and none block current tests. The Pydantic V1→V2 migration is the most consequential — it should be bundled with Knowledge Compiler V2 ratification to avoid touching /do_next twice.
